structures.name: Contrarian Insight
structures.format: Hook (bold claim) → Context (2-3 sentences) → Evidence (3 bullets) → Takeaway → Question
example_outline: Context: Why most people believe the opposite
Evidence: 3 data points or observations supporting your view
Takeaway: What this means for readers

structures.name: Story to Lesson
structures.description: Share a narrative that reveals an insight
structures.format: Hook → Brief narrative (3-4 sentences) → Turning point → Lesson learned → Call to action
example_outline: Narrative: Set the scene, what happened
Turning point: The moment of realization
CTA: What readers should do or consider

structures.name: Data Driven
structures.description: Lead with numbers, build to synthesis
structures.format: Surprising statistic → Breakdown (3 numbered points) → Synthesis → Question
example_outline: Stat: Open with a compelling number
Point 1: First implication of the data
Synthesis: What it all means together

structures.name: Framework Share
structures.description: Teach a mental model or decision framework
structures.format: Problem statement → Framework intro → Steps (3-4 numbered) → Real example → Invitation to try
example_outline: Framework: Name and introduce your approach
Invitation: Encourage readers to apply it

structures.name: Prediction
structures.description: Make a bold call with supporting signals
structures.format: Bold prediction → Current signals (3 bullets) → Historical parallel → What to watch → Caveat
example_outline: Historical parallel: When something similar happened

structures.name: Then vs Now
structures.description: Compare two time periods or scenarios
structures.format: Setup (then) → Contrast (now) → Key differences (bullets) → Implications → Question

structures.name: Myth vs Reality
structures.format: Common belief → Why it's wrong → The reality (with evidence) → What to do instead → Question
